subject: btrtl: add RTL8761B ROMs to list.
author: Sorgelig

impact.effect_if_absent: None (functional) / cosmetic (metadata) — MODULE_FIRMWARE(_firmware) expands to MODULE_INFO(firmware, _firmware) (/mnt/source/linux/include/linux/module.h:305), which only embeds a modinfo tag readable via `modinfo`/depmod/initramfs-building tools (dracut, mkinitramfs); it is NOT consulted by the runtime firmware loader. The kernel's actual request_firmware() call (drivers/bluetooth/btrtl.c:883, `request_firmware(&fw, name, &hdev->dev)`) resolves the RTL8761B firmware by the `.fw_name`/`.cfg_name` strings hardcoded in the `ic_id_table[]` id_table entry, which the fork already had BEFORE this commit (`.fw_name = "rtl_bt/rtl8761b_fw.bin"`, present since the v5.13.12 base-import commit e12ed6c19, confirmed via `git log -S` in Linux-Kernel_MiSTer). So RTL8761B firmware loading works identically with or without these two MODULE_FIRMWARE lines. MiSTer's target build has no initramfs (BR2_TARGET_ROOTFS_EXT2 direct ext4 rootfs, not cpio-based) and Buildroot's linux-firmware package does not derive its file list from kernel-module modinfo — firmware inclusion is driven entirely by explicit BR2_PACKAGE_LINUX_FIRMWARE_* options in the defconfig. Confirmed our built image already ships rtl_bt/rtl8761b_fw.bin, rtl_bt/rtl8761b_config.bin (and the rtl8761bu variants) directly at output/target/usr/lib/firmware/rtl_bt/, via BR2_PACKAGE_LINUX_FIRMWARE_RTL_87XX_BT=y (configs/mister_de10nano_defconfig:651), independent of any MODULE_FIRMWARE declaration. Net effect of dropping this commit: `modinfo btrtl` would omit two lines; zero effect on device function.

notes: MiSTer v5.13.12 backport (Aug 2021) of functionality later added to vanilla upstream (May 2023, bb23f07cb639). Not carried forward to MiSTer-v5.15 branch. Upstream patch is more comprehensive (also adds rtl8761bu, rtl8723d, rtl8723cs_vf, rtl8821c, rtl8821cs, rtl8822cs, rtl8822cu, rtl8851bu variants), but the rtl8761b_fw.bin and rtl8761b_config.bin declaration lines this fork commit added are byte-identical to bb23f07cb639's hunk (verified: `git show bb23f07cb63975968bbabe314486e2b087234fc5 -- drivers/bluetooth/btrtl.c` in /mnt/source/linux; SHA confirmed to exist and is an ancestor of v6.18.38 via `git merge-base --is-ancestor`). Line numbers re-verified against current HEAD checkout: both fork and vanilla now sit at btrtl.c:1523-1524. Commit existed only on old v5.13 branch, not on stock v5.15 branch, so was not carried forward during version bump. CORRECTION (sonnet re-verification): the original record's impact claim ("devices won't function without the declarations") was WRONG. MODULE_FIRMWARE() is a MODULE_INFO() modinfo-only macro (include/linux/module.h:305) with no effect on the request_firmware() runtime path — the actual firmware filename used at runtime comes from the `.fw_name`/`.cfg_name` fields in btrtl.c's id_table, which predate this commit. Verified our build ships the rtl8761b firmware files directly (output/target/usr/lib/firmware/rtl_bt/rtl8761b_fw.bin, rtl8761b_config.bin exist, dated Oct 10 2025, via BR2_PACKAGE_LINUX_FIRMWARE_RTL_87XX_BT). Severity downgraded feature-loss -> cosmetic and effect_if_absent rewritten to reflect that this is a metadata-only gap (modinfo output), not a functional regression. Equivalence upgraded partial -> equivalent since the specific 2-line delta this commit introduces is byte-identical upstream (upstream's commit is broader in scope, covering unrelated chips, but that doesn't diminish equivalence of what THIS commit changed). Disposition (dropped-upstream) and vanilla_shas/vanilla_quote/vanilla_file_line were already correct and unchanged. sonnet-verified: corrected — impact/severity was overstated (MODULE_FIRMWARE is modinfo metadata, not required for request_firmware() at runtime; our .fw_name id_table entry and shipped firmware files predate/are independent of this commit), downgraded feature-loss -> cosmetic; equivalence sharpened partial -> equivalent; SHA/quote/line-number grounding re-confirmed against live /mnt/source/linux checkout.
